name: Sundae Pies (Crust Recipes, Ice Cream Fillings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick a Crust:
- SPICY-PASTRY CRUST: To half pkg. piecrust mix, add 2 tablespoons sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Make into 8 inches or 9 inches pie shell as package directs; bake. Or make favorite pastry and bake in 8 inches or 9 inches pie shell.
- COCONUT-CHOCOLATE CRUST: In double boiler, melt 2 squares unsweetened chocolate with 2 tablespoons butter or margarine. Blend. Stir in 2/3 cup sifted confectioners sugar mixed with 2 tablespoons hot milk; mix in 1 1/2 cups snipped shredded coconut. Press to bottom and sides of greased 8 inches pie plate. Refrigerate to make firm. (Let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes before filling.)
- GRAHAM-CRACKER CRUST: Mix 1 1/3 cups graham-cracker crumbs with 1/4 cup granulated sugar and 1/4 cup soft butter or margarine until crumbly. Press to bottom and sides of 9 inches pie plate. Bake at 375°F for 8 minutes.
- GINGERSNAP CRUST: Mix 1 1/3 cups gingersnap crumbs with 6 tablespoons soft butter or margarine until crumbly. Press to bottom, sides, 9 inches pie plate. Bake at 375°F for 8 minutes.
- CHOCOLATE-CRUMB CRUST: Mix 1 1/3 cups chocolate-wafer crumbs with 3 tablespoons soft butter or margarine until crumbly. Press to bottom and sides of 9 inches pie plate. Bake at 375°F for 8 minutes.
- MERINGUE CRUST: Sift 1/2 cup granulated sugar with 1/8 teaspoon cream of tartar. Beat 2 egg whites until stiff but not dry; gradually add sugar, continuing to beat until stiff and glossy. Use to line bottom and sides of well-greased 9 inches pie plate (up to but not on rim), hollowing out center to make bottom crust about 1/4 inch thick. Bake at 275°F for 1 hour. Cool.
2. Fill with Ice Cream:
- At serving time, heap crust shell with ice cream that's complementary in color and flavor (1 to 2 pints for 8 inches shell; 2 to 3 pints for 9 inches shell). Or use coconut or nuts.
- Try: Chocolate-Crumb Crust, vanilla ice cream, chocolate sauce, whipped cream; Gingersnap Crust, vanilla ice cream, canned whole-cranberry sauce; Graham-Cracker Crust, peach ice cream, slivered almonds or Melba Sauce; Spicy-Pastry Crust, coffee ice cream, butterscotch sauce, shaved chocolate; Coconut-Chocolate Crust, cherry-vanilla ice cream; Meringue Crust, vanilla ice cream, sliced strawberries.
3. For Baked-Alaska Pie:
- Choose 9 inches Spicy-Pastry, Graham-Cracker, Gingersnap, or Chocolate-Crumb crust (above). Refrigerate until well chilled. Start heating oven to 500°F. Make meringue as in Step 2 of Baked Alaska (page 101). Working quickly, fill shell well with 1 quart firm ice cream. Cover entire surface of ice cream with meringue. Bake about 2 minutes, or until delicate brown. Serve at once.
